content.claim: Germany's post-reunification adoption of tripartite tracking in East German states led to sharp rise in educational inequality after previously running unified comprehensive schools (POS)
content.context: Fresh live research through the International comparison lens
limitations: Natural experiment confounded by massive institutional transition (GDR to FRG) with multiple simultaneous changes
No isolating study measures tracking adoption effect separately from other post-reunification reforms
Infrastructure and teacher reallocation factors may explain inequality rise independently of tracking mechanism
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
